cap_bit: RXGD_CAP_FUSED_POST_CHAIN_REAL_PASS (1 << 13) (PATCH_ALLOCATION.md section 3 reservation; NOT wired in this slice)

fusion_members.note: Both fusion members recorded strict MEASURED opt-in real-pass success in their own slices (the fusion precondition); referencing them makes NO new claim for this pass.

member_a.fused_scope: final WRITE_LUMINANCE level only (tile mean -> clamp -> EMA -> 1x1 current); upper pyramid levels stay native

member_b.fused_scope: TONEMAPPER_LINEAR + linear_to_srgb subset (existing member kernel math), with the exposure input upgraded to the fused in-kernel luminance current per tonemap.glsl L866-868

fusion_members.key_gap_fixed_by_fusion: the native tonemap reads the luminance current value through the exposure_texture sampler (renderer_scene_render_rd.cpp:697), but patch 0012 forwards only scalar exposure/white/luminance_multiplier with no exposure_texture handle, so the existing Rurix tonemap pass is semantically correct only with auto exposure OFF; the fused kernel makes the register-carried luminance current the tonemap exposure input (native L866-868 formula), covering the auto-exposure-ON scenario

godot_hook_investigation.note: Investigation only; external/godot-master is NOT modified. Paths and functions are recorded for the deferred patch 0036 call-site gate and the patch 0037 native-handle mapping.

call_sites.region: auto-exposure luminance_reduction (L555-588 region, set_immediate/step) followed by the tonemap block (exposure_texture = get_current_luminance_buffer at L697, use_auto_exposure/auto_exposure_scale L698-700, DEFAULT_RD_TEXTURE_WHITE fallback L702)
call_sites.note: deferred patch 0036 would gate this seam; the native luminance_reduction + tonemap chain is always preserved as fallback/continuation (two-level fallback contract).

member_functions: RendererRD::Luminance::luminance_reduction (luminance.cpp L159-256; final WRITE_LUMINANCE level L228-231; SWAP(current, reduce[last]) L255)
RendererRD::ToneMapper::tonemapper (tone_mapper.cpp; u_exposure_texture set 1 binding 0 L183-187; TONEMAP_FLAG_USE_AUTO_EXPOSURE / auto_exposure_scale L155/L158)
shaders: servers/rendering/renderer_rd/shaders/effects/luminance_reduce.glsl (WRITE_LUMINANCE L76-79: avg = clamp(prev + (avg - prev) * exposure_adjust, min, max) - EMA inside the clamp)
servers/rendering/renderer_rd/shaders/effects/tonemap.glsl (L860 luminance_multiplier, L864-870 exposure with the auto-exposure leg L866-868, L893 apply_tonemapping LINEAR, L942-943 linear_to_srgb)
godot_hook_investigation.native_pass_shape: two native passes fused into ONE full-resolution compute dispatch design: the luminance final level (source <= 8x8 after the native reduce chain, 1x1 output) rides as a per-thread-group register prologue; the tonemap segment defines the full-res thread grid. The native chain between them (gaussian_glow) is NOT fused and glow-on scenes fail the fusion precondition.

descriptor_mapping.root_constant_shape_note: merged member b0s + fusion controls; [i64 x4, f32 x8] keeps the canonical dims-first packing discipline but intentionally departs from the members' 28-byte shape - the deferred S4 gate must validate 64 bytes
descriptor_mapping.dst_shape: dst_color extent == src_color extent (1:1 full-resolution); dst_luminance and prev_luminance extents == 1x1 (different resources, double-buffered mirroring the native SWAP); lum_source extent <= 8x8 (single tile)

supported_math_subset.variant: single fused full-resolution dispatch: luminance final WRITE_LUMINANCE level (segment A, per-group register prologue) + tonemap TONEMAPPER_LINEAR/linear_to_srgb (segment B)
supported_math_subset.segment_a: partial-tile-correct mean over lum_source (<= 8x8, member-kernel accumulation order at destination texel (0,0)); cur = clamp(avg, min_luminance, max_luminance); ema = prev + (cur - prev) * exposure_adjust; first_frame != 0 selects cur; redundant per-group recompute + groupshared broadcast; dispatch group (0,0) thread 0 writes dst_luminance[0,0]
supported_math_subset.segment_b: color.rgb *= luminance_multiplier; exposure_effective = exposure * (1.0 / (lum_current * luminance_multiplier / auto_exposure_scale)) with lum_current register-carried from segment A (tonemap.glsl L866-868 operation order); color.rgb *= exposure_effective; LINEAR identity; linear_to_srgb (tonemap.glsl L230-233); alpha passthrough
supported_math_subset.precision: float32 kernel; parity uses float32 with per-op binary32 rounding

known_gaps: glow composite not covered (pre/post/softlight/mix modes, glow map, bicubic upscale; gaussian_glow's own luminance_texture + auto_exposure_scale consumption stays outside the fusion) - glow-on scenes fail the fusion precondition
tonemapper modes other than LINEAR (Reinhard/FILMIC/ACES/AgX) unsupported

auto-exposure texture chain elsewhere not covered: only the tonemap-side exposure_texture read is fused; the upper luminance reduce pyramid levels (READ_TEXTURE level 0 + intermediates) must still be produced by the native chain, and other exposure_texture consumers stay native

inherited member clamp-order divergence: segment A mirrors the GRX-009 tracked kernel (clamp then EMA); native WRITE_LUMINANCE is clamp(prev + (avg - prev) * adjust) (EMA inside the clamp) - bounded transient divergence, steady state agrees

first_frame=1 semantics: this kernel outputs cur = clamp(avg); the native p_set final level runs a plain reduce writing the RAW avg (no clamp, no EMA) - divergence bounded by the clamp, documented (differs from the member's zero-prev cur*exposure_adjust degeneration)

lum_current == 0 (min_luminance = 0 on an all-black scene) makes exposure_effective divide to inf - reproduced faithfully per native GLSL divide semantics, no extra guard; parity fixtures use min_luminance > 0
rgba16f / r32f storage quantization not modelled (kernel and parity compute float32; the native color buffer is half)

one-frame latency: the luminance segment's EMA feedback is engineering-defensible with previous-frame inputs (GRX-009 declaration reused); the tonemap segment natively consumes the CURRENT frame's internal texture, so a self-queue fused dispatch cannot substitute the frame's real tonemap output - a true replacement needs draw_graph route B (RenderingShaderContainerD3D12 nativization is the recorded long-term direction); this slice stops at offline+parity
sRGB output is not clamped to [0,1] (inherited from the tonemap member; native path relies on framebuffer quantization)
raster-vs-compute output seam: Godot writes the LDR result via a fullscreen fragment pass; this kernel writes a full-res UAV (inherited from the tonemap member)
lum_source extent > 8x8 unsupported (single-tile constraint mirroring the native final-level 1x1-dst shape); the deferred S4 gate must fail closed
no FXAA, no BCS, no color correction, no debanding, no multiview, no HDR output (convert_to_srgb=false) (inherited from the tonemap member)
GPU-side math parity observation pending a real dispatch (math_parity_evidence.json)

two_level_contract.level_1: fusion gate failure -> per-member single-pass gated paths (existing LuminanceReductionGate / TonemapGate opt-in arms, themselves fallback by default); fusion-level marker must be distinguishable from member-level markers (patch slice 0036-0038, deferred)
two_level_contract.level_2: member gate failure -> native Godot luminance_reduction + tonemap

compile_blockers.summary: the canonical fused_post_chain package is the owner-approved hlsl_bridge_workaround artifact (DXC cs_6_0 container validated by dxv, per-slot texture2d/rwtexture2d binding kinds, Rurix-owned RTS0; the GRX-009 texture_artifact_provenance_policy.json applies to every texture compute pass); a rurixc-owned fused_post_chain compile still requires a patched llc that supports texture intrinsics plus multi-channel texture element support and groupshared lang items in the Rurix toolchain

implementation_status.runtime_state_note: The DEFAULT runtime path stays fallback-only: with all per-pass settings at their false defaults the bridge still returns RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK for RXGD_PASS_FUSED_POST_CHAIN and the native Godot luminance_reduction + tonemap chain renders. real_gpu_pass=true / real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded=true record the strict MEASURED success of the opt-in real-pass arm (dispatch_real_pass under a d3d12-recording-shim bridge on the 0001..0026 + 0036..0038 scratch build); it is not a default enablement and carries no performance claim. The shipping feature-off bridge still fails closed with real_dispatch_path_not_linked, and the patch 0038 result writeback is a SCAFFOLD (native Godot luminance_reduction + tonemap chain stays the continuation/backstop and re-renders every frame, so the candidate image cannot change and no net benefit exists yet). The fused binding itself is a scaffold (lum_source/prev_luminance alias the single public 1x1 current luminance buffer; dst_color/dst_luminance target the LDR tonemap destination the native tonemapper fully overwrites), so the strict success asserts ONLY that a real D3D12 fused dispatch was recorded and the native-continuation visual gate stayed bit-stable, NOT fused-output correctness. No estimated fused_post_chain GPU time is attributed.
implementation_status.bridge_gate: src/rurix-godot FusedPostChainGate (already in lib.rs): runtime binding preflight (exactly 5 texture resources src_color/lum_source/prev_luminance/dst_color/dst_luminance, 64-byte b0, nonzero source dims and texture extents; NOTE the implemented preflight does NOT check int64) -> dispatch eligibility (RXGD_CAP_FUSED_POST_CHAIN_REAL_PASS (1 << 13) opt-in, RXGD_CAP_SHADER_INT64, non-null D3D12 device/queue, non-null native handles, FusedPostChainDispatchPackage layout/digest match vs offline evidence) -> per-slot kernel-binding-kind conformance ([texture2d x3, rwtexture2d x2]) -> math-parity gate (fused_post_chain_cpu_reference_proven_pending_gpu_dispatch) -> real dispatch only under the d3d12-recording-shim feature; every failure records a fallback reason and prints the once-per-session RXGD_FUSED_POST_CHAIN_REAL_PASS_BLOCKED diagnostic. Because int64 is NOT in the preflight, the forced capability downgrade fails closed one level later at dispatch_eligibility_failed/unsupported_device (not runtime_binding_preflight_failed like the other texture passes), measured in the enablement smoke forced leg.
implementation_status.godot_module_patch: spike/godot-rurix/patches/0036-rurix-accel-fused-post-chain-pass-gate-and-callsite.patch (stacked on 0001..0026; default-false rendering/rurix_accel/passes/fused_post_chain/enabled setting, try_record_fused_post_chain() module gate, default-false virtual D3D12Hooks::try_record_fused_post_chain(), and the FUSION-FIRST call-site opt-in gate in renderer_scene_render_rd.cpp _render_buffers_post_process_and_tonemap that runs BEFORE the per-member single-pass tonemap gate so the two-level fallback resolves fusion -> member -> native; the native luminance_reduction + tonemap chain is always preserved as fallback/continuation). The runtime native-handle binding is patch 0037-rurix-accel-fused-post-chain-runtime-resource-binding.patch and the recording-smoke + real-pass opt-in arms are patch 0038-rurix-accel-fused-post-chain-recording-smoke-and-real-pass-optin.patch. fused_post_chain 0036-0038 stack on the 0026 tip; 0027-0035 are reserved for GRX-015/016/018 (a legal monotonic hole, PATCH_ALLOCATION.md section 4 rule 2).

wired_call_sites.note: the FUSION-FIRST opt-in gate calls D3D12Hooks::try_record_fused_post_chain() before the per-member single-pass tonemap gate (patch 0013) and before the native tonemapper, wiring the two-level fallback (fusion -> member -> native). Handles: src_color = HDR internal color texture, dst_color = LDR tonemap destination (both real, resolved via RenderingDevice::get_driver_resource), lum_source = prev_luminance = the public 1x1 current luminance buffer (read-only), dst_luminance = the LDR tonemap destination (scaffold, native overwrites). A distinct, correct fused binding needs the deferred Luminance-API extension / draw_graph route B (PASS_CONTRACT.md 3.4/5). applyability verified via git apply --check on a scratch copy; the 0001..0026 + 0036..0038 scratch Godot compiled clean.

standalone_d3d12_dispatch_smoke.purpose: Prove the tracked offline fused_post_chain DXIL container + RTS0 root signature + descriptor layout can complete one minimal compute dispatch on a real D3D12 device/command queue with five textures (SRV t0..t2 + UAV u0..u1) AND that the measured GPU output matches the CPU reference within tolerance. Standalone measured evidence only; the bridge records no dispatch and returns no OK.
does_not_imply: any visual diff, measured fallback telemetry, or performance claim

real_pass_measured_success.real_gpu_pass_scope: opt_in_only: the measured real dispatch requires the default-false dispatch_real_pass arm plus a d3d12-recording-shim bridge DLL; the default runtime path stays fallback-only and the shipping feature-off bridge fails closed with real_dispatch_path_not_linked
real_pass_measured_success.pass_enable_matrix: three legs measured on the 0001..0026 + 0036..0038 scratch build: disabled_default (reference, zero markers) / enabled_real_pass_optin (candidate, real_pass_marker + writeback_marker + record_marker observed, RXGD_GODOT_RUNTIME_FUSED_POST_CHAIN_REAL_PASS recorded=1) / forced_capability_downgrade (RXGD_FUSED_POST_CHAIN_REAL_PASS_BLOCKED first_missing_prerequisite=dispatch_eligibility_failed fallback_reason=unsupported_device)
real_pass_measured_success.scene: deterministic 3D scene with CameraAttributesPractical.auto_exposure_enabled=true (drives the native luminance_reduction so the 1x1 current luminance buffer is valid, the fusion call site's luminance input) + FILMIC tonemapper; fixed_fps makes the auto-exposure temporal EMA converge deterministically across legs; capture frame 40 at 256x144
real_pass_measured_success.ldr_visual_gate: max_abs=0 / mean_abs=0 (candidate is bit-exact to the reference because the writeback is a scaffold and the native luminance_reduction + tonemap continuation re-renders every frame; the fused dispatch writes only the LDR destination the native tonemapper overwrites and never the live luminance buffer)
real_pass_measured_success.does_not_imply_math_correctness: the strict success asserts only that a real D3D12 fused dispatch was recorded and the native-continuation visual gate stayed bit-stable; the lum_source/prev/dst bindings are a scaffold (PASS_CONTRACT.md 3.4/5) and no fused-output correctness or net-benefit claim is made

remaining_segments: distinct/correct fused binding via a Luminance-API extension (exposing the <=8x8 reduce level and the double-buffered 1x1 prev/dst luminance targets) OR draw_graph route B (RenderingShaderContainerD3D12 nativization) so the tonemap segment's same-frame output can truly be replaced
glow, tonemapper modes other than LINEAR (Reinhard/FILMIC/ACES/AgX), and the rest of the auto-exposure consumer chain
per-pass FPS baseline comparison (contract requires >= 0.95x baseline before any default-enable discussion)

rd_native.summary: GRX Route B fused_post_chain rd_native is the FUSION-FIRST leg of the two-level fallback (fused rd_native -> single-pass tonemap rd_native (patch 0040) -> native). It is SELF-CONTAINED on the culling tail (the fused shim scaffold 0036-0038 is on the mutually-exclusive fused tail and NOT in this stack), with its own RD_NATIVE_FUSED_POST_CHAIN_* 64-byte/5-resource constants. Bridge-independent; the shim FUSED_POST_CHAIN_REAL_PASS bit 13 is unrelated. REVISED IN PLACE (patch 0045, no number change) to the Design 1 shadow-recompute, unlocked by the patch 0048 Luminance-API read-only getters: the call site now supplies DISTINCT current / previous / penultimate luminance buffers, the fused kernel reads two distinct luminance SRVs, and the module REDIRECTS the fused luminance-final WRITE (u1) to a self-owned 1x1 R32F STORAGE scratch that is never read back. The fused gate also ANDs the shared rurix_tonemap_linear_subset_compatible predicate from patch 0040 (LINEAR + convert_to_srgb + no color effects; auto exposure allowed here). It now GENUINELY RECORDS when auto-exposure produced distinct luminance buffers, and still fails closed (cascading to tonemap rd_native) when they alias.

backend_states.0: disabled (default; tonemap rd_native / native chain unchanged)
backend_states.1: reserved (no shim path on this tail; behaves as disabled)
backend_states.2: rd_native (engage the fusion-first replacement, cascading to tonemap rd_native)

rd_native.patch_stack: 0001-0029 + 0040..0045 (branch-HEAD culling tail + rd_native series; the build also needs 0046-0048, and 0045 depends on the 0048 Luminance-API getters)
rd_native.hook_virtual: D3D12Hooks::try_record_fused_post_chain_rd_native(RID p_src_color, RID p_lum_source, RID p_prev_luminance, RID p_dst_color, RID p_dst_luminance, Size2i p_source_size, Size2i p_dest_size, float p_exposure, float p_white, float p_luminance_multiplier)
rd_native.descriptor_mapping: five textures: src_color=t0 SRV, lum_source=current-luminance t1 SRV, prev_luminance=previous-luminance t2 SRV, dst_color=u0 UAV, dst_luminance=u1 UAV bound to a SELF-OWNED 1x1 R32F STORAGE scratch (NOT the call site's p_dst_luminance penultimate handle, which is passed only to keep a valid distinct argument); 64-byte b0 [i64 source dims, i64 lum_source dims, f32 max/min_luminance, exposure_adjust, exposure, white, luminance_multiplier, first_frame, auto_exposure_scale]; dispatch ceil(dest/8) over the container 8x8 local size; own compute_list_begin/end.

enablement.measured_note: Re-signed on the 0001-0029+0040-0048 rb3 scratch Godot (Windows D3D12 Forward+, RTX 4070 Ti), 5-leg matrix, RURIX_REQUIRE_REAL=1. With auto exposure driven correctly via CameraAttributesPractical (the patch 0048 getters now return DISTINCT current/previous luminance buffers), the candidate leg (backend=2 + real container) GENUINELY ENGAGES the fused rd_native replacement: RXGD_RD_NATIVE_FUSED_POST_CHAIN active appears in the candidate leg ONLY, the native luminance-final + tonemap end is skipped, the fail_closed leg (garbage container) latches the container-reject and is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the native AE reference, and the cascade leg prints RXGD_RD_NATIVE_TONEMAP active in that leg ONLY and is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the non-AE reference (LDR max_abs=0). HONEST MEASURED FINDING: the fused kernel's LDR output diverges from Godot's native auto-exposure tonemap by LDR max_abs=85 / mean_abs=66 (thresholds max<=4 / mean<=1.0) — the FIRST real-hardware comparison of the fused AE/EMA + tonemap math. The fused kernel's b0 auto-exposure scalars are best-effort placeholders (max_luminance=1, min_luminance=0, exposure_adjust=1, first_frame=0, auto_exposure_scale=1) pending the Luminance-API extension supplying the real fused parameters, so the exposure the fused kernel applies differs from the native AE feedback. This is an honest measured_prerequisite_blocked (fused_tonemap_parity_out_of_tolerance), NOT a strict success and NOT beautified; it does not advance the gate and no rd_native_enablement_success_evidence.json is written.
enablement.historical_misattribution_note: The prior 'fused gate blocked at the aliasing guard / fused_luminance_double_buffer_api_unexposed, marker absent in every leg, candidate byte-identical to reference' result was a MISATTRIBUTION adjudicated by the main session: the prior smoke scene assigned auto_exposure_enabled on Environment (which this Godot build does not expose), so every run raised a GDScript SCRIPT ERROR and auto exposure NEVER engaged — the luminance buffers were never allocated, the patch 0048 getters returned RID(), and the module failed closed at the invalid-lum-RID check (NOT the aliasing guard). The runtime audit missed it because it scanned only 'ERROR:' prefixes and ignored 'SCRIPT ERROR' lines. The smoke now (a) enables AE via CameraAttributesPractical (mirroring ci/grx009_segment4h) and (b) fails on SCRIPT ERROR lines, so this re-sign is the first honest engage+parity measurement.
enablement.revision_note: Patch 0045 Design 1 revision + patch 0048 Luminance-API getters unlock a genuine engage on the AE candidate leg; the re-run confirms the engage but records an out-of-tolerance parity (max_abs=85 / mean_abs=66) as an honest measured gap. No strict success is claimed; success evidence files are NOT hand-edited.

remaining_rounds: Resolve the fused kernel's auto-exposure b0 parameters (currently best-effort placeholders: max_luminance=1, min_luminance=0, exposure_adjust=1, first_frame=0, auto_exposure_scale=1) so the candidate leg's LDR output matches Godot's native auto-exposure tonemap within the parity thresholds (measured gap LDR max_abs=85 / mean_abs=66), then re-run grx_rb_fused_post_chain_rd_native for a strict success. The engage path itself is now proven (marker in the candidate leg only; native tonemap skipped).
Design 2 true fusion (glow-off gate + skip the native final reduce level + external SWAP of the luminance double-buffer) where a structural dispatch saving could be claimed (deferred, later batch).

honest_boundary.real_replacement_scope: only the LINEAR + convert_to_srgb tonemap leg (t0 -> u0); the luminance-final leg (t1,t2 -> u1) is a dead-end scratch write, never read
honest_boundary.note: This is NOT a true fusion. The fused kernel's tonemap output IS the frame (identical to the single-pass tonemap rd_native kernel), but its luminance-final recompute is discarded to scratch and the native luminance_reduction pyramid still runs every frame, so there is NO dispatch or work saving. A genuine fusion (Design 2: glow-off gate + skip the native final reduce level + external SWAP of the luminance double-buffer) is a later batch and is where a structural saving could be claimed.

rd_native.godot_patch_sha256_note: 0045 revised in place (Design 1 shadow-recompute); this sha reflects the revised patch file. The strict enablement re-run on the rb3 scratch build (0001-0029+0040-0048) confirmed the fused gate ENGAGES on the AE candidate leg but measured an out-of-tolerance tonemap parity (LDR max_abs=85 / mean_abs=66), so no rd_native_enablement_success_evidence.json is written; success evidence files are NOT hand-edited.

evidence_requirements.visual: in-engine LDR absolute diff (GRX-009 segment 4g template) is a later slice; not produced here
evidence_requirements.perf: reuse GRX-006 baseline/perf gate; no dispatch/barrier/VRAM-traffic or perf improvement claimed

disclaimer: GRX-019 fused_post_chain is closed out at the stage-A5-equivalent level (mirroring GRX-010/013): the pass contract trio, the DXC/DXV/RTS0 offline fused kernel package (hlsl_bridge_workaround provenance, runtime-mappable per the owner-approved GRX-009 texture artifact provenance policy), the CPU float32 math parity reference (EMA time series x tonemap composite, pending_gpu_dispatch), the fail-closed bridge FusedPostChainGate (default RXGD_STATUS_FALLBACK; opt-in RXGD_CAP_FUSED_POST_CHAIN_REAL_PASS arm), the Godot patch stack 0001..0026 + 0036..0038 (patches 0036-0038: per-pass settings default false + FUSION-FIRST call-site opt-in gate + runtime native-handle binding + recording-smoke/real-pass opt-in; native luminance_reduction + tonemap chain always preserved as the two-level fallback), the standalone real D3D12 dispatch smoke, and the gated real-pass enablement gate that recorded a strict MEASURED success (real_pass_enablement_success_evidence.json, strict_success=true). implemented=true / real_gpu_pass=true / real_d3d12_dispatch_recorded=true record the MEASURED opt-in real-pass arm only. The DEFAULT path stays fallback-only, default_enable_state stays disabled per the owner decision (real_pass_default_enable_decision.json, keep_default_disabled), the shipping feature-off bridge still fails closed with real_dispatch_path_not_linked, the patch 0038 writeback is a SCAFFOLD (native Godot luminance_reduction + tonemap continuation/backstop re-renders every frame; candidate image bit-exact, max_abs=0/mean_abs=0), and the fused binding itself is a SCAFFOLD (lum_source/prev_luminance alias the single public 1x1 current luminance buffer, dst_color/dst_luminance target the LDR tonemap destination the native tonemapper overwrites), so the strict success asserts ONLY that a real D3D12 fused dispatch was recorded and the native-continuation visual gate stayed bit-stable. NO fused-output correctness, visual-superiority, GPU-timestamp, FPS, or performance improvement is claimed; a distinct/correct fused binding needs the deferred Luminance-API extension / draw_graph route B. The one-dispatch fusion motivation is a structural design statement, not a measured result.
